title: rm -rf with dynamic timestamp path in directory_layout_bridge — TOCTOU risk under stale flock
evidence: rm -rf .opencode/skill_legacy_backup_$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ) 2>/dev/null constructs a path from date output. If the flock is released prematurely (SIGKILL), a subsequent concurrent invocation could delete a legitimate backup directory.
scopeProof: rg -n 'rm -rf' doctor_update.yaml shows single site at line 287
recommendation: Add a second flock re-check immediately before rm -rf, OR use a fixed backup path instead of timestamped path

title: npm install --no-audit suppresses vulnerability scanning during bootstrap
evidence: npm install --no-audit --no-fund --silent skips the npm audit step, hiding known vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies before the MCP server starts
recommendation: Remove --no-audit or emit warning when npm audit reports high/critical vulnerabilities

title: cocoindex_venv_check runs pip install -e . on local path — executes package setup.py
evidence: pip install --quiet -e .opencode/skills/mcp-coco-index/mcp_server runs arbitrary Python code in setup.py/pyproject.toml during editable install
scopeProof: rg -n 'pip install.*-e' doctor_update.yaml confirms single site at line 307
recommendation: Add hash/signature verification of CocoIndex setup.py before pip install, or warn operator

title: mkdir lock primitive is TOCTOU-raceable — bootstrap runs before flock acquisition
evidence: Bootstrap mkdir lock at line 129 serializes only the bootstrap process. doctor_update.yaml Phase 1 flock at line 205 is AFTER Phase 0 bootstrap completes. Two concurrent OpenCode sessions could both detect missing dist and race into npm install.
scopeProof: bootstrap.sh line 50 defines LOCK_DIR, line 129 acquires, line 100 releases on trap. doctor_update.yaml line 116 references .doctor-update.bootstrap.lockdir but no flock on it; Phase 0 bootstrap runs BEFORE Phase 1 flock at line 205
recommendation: Move flock acquisition to before Phase 0 bootstrap, or have bootstrap script acquire .doctor-update.flock

title: No integrity verification of migration-manifest.json before executing migration operations
evidence: YAML instructs reading migration-manifest.json without hash/signature/schema validation. Corrupted manifest could declare incorrect legacy file paths for --cleanup-legacy.
scopeProof: rg -n 'migration-manifest.json' update.md doctor_update.yaml confirms 3 references, none include hash verification
recommendation: Add JSON schema validation or SHA-256 hash check of migration-manifest.json before acting on its contents

title: mcp-doctor.sh reads .env and .utcp_config.json metadata — confirms environmental shape
evidence: Script checks .env file existence and .utcp_config.json parse-ability. Does not log contents, but confirms which config files exist.
scopeProof: rg -n '.env|.utcp_config' mcp-doctor.sh shows references at lines 387-395
recommendation: Document in help text that diagnostic reads filesystem metadata only, not file contents. No code change needed.

title: --root flag accepts arbitrary filesystem paths without workspace-boundary validation
evidence: ROOT is accepted verbatim, canonicalized via cd+pwd, only validated by checking .opencode/ exists. Operator could pass --root /tmp/evil with malicious package.json.
scopeProof: rg -n '--root|ROOT_OVERRIDE|ROOT=' doctor-runtime-bootstrap.sh shows usage at lines 10-16, 39-42, 123
recommendation: Add git worktree check or validate presence of expected marker files beyond .opencode/

evidence: REQ-005→yaml:205, REQ-006→yaml:369, REQ-011→yaml:459. Missing: REQ-022 cleanup command not explicit in yaml:483
